commentaries.author: Douay Rheims 1609
commentaries.content:
But (a) Jabes invocated the God of Israel, saying: If blessing thou wilt bless me, and wilt enlarge my borders, and thy hand be with me, and thou wilt make that I be not oppressed by malice. And God granted the things that he prayed for.
(a) In joining a vow to his prayer he imitated holy Jacob. Gen. 28. And they both desired temporal things for the better serving of God, & advancing his Glory; especially that they might be assisted with grace not to yield to temptations, nor sin of malice.

commentaries.author: George Leo Haydock
commentaries.content: Fear and reverence for his master, (Calmet) as well as concern for his own safety. (Haydock)
